name: Sparrow Quantum

notes: Niels Bohr Institute spin-off (Peter Lodahl); HQ in Frederiksberg (Copenhagen metro); raised EUR 27.5M Series A (2025).

name: Spectrum Instrumentation
description: Arbitrary waveform generators and digitizers for quantum control and readout.

name: Stable Laser Systems
description: Ultra-stable lasers and Fabry-Perot reference cavities for optical clocks and quantum experiments.
notes: JILA/NIST-linked; Boulder, CO.

name: Stirling Cryogenics
description: Stirling-cycle cryocoolers and stand-alone cryogenic cooling systems.

name: STMicroelectronics
description: Semiconductor manufacturer with process capabilities relevant to qubit and quantum chip fabrication.
notes: HQ Plan-les-Ouates (Geneva); incorporated in the Netherlands.

name: Sumitomo Heavy Industries (SHI Cryogenics)
description: Gifford-McMahon and pulse-tube cryocoolers and cryogenic refrigeration for quantum and research systems.
notes: SHI Cryogenics Group HQ in Shinagawa-ku, Tokyo.

name: sureCore
description: Licenses low-power and cryogenic (CryoMem) CMOS memory/IP for cryo control electronics in quantum computing.
notes: Led GBP 6.5M Innovate UK cryo-CMOS consortium; founded as low-power memory IP specialist.

name: Sweden Quantum
description: Produces ultra-low-loss infrared-blocking filters (HERD technology) for superconducting quantum computing.
notes: Chalmers/WACQT spin-off (2022); co-founders Simone Gasparinetti and Robert Rehammar.

name: SynthBits
description: Develops chemically synthesized transition-metal molecular qubits for computing, communication and nanoscale sensing.
notes: Argonne Chain Reaction Innovations (Cohort 8) company; founded by Berk Diler Kovos (PhD, UChicago PME). No standalone website confirmed; initially targeting quantum microscopy.

name: Tabor Electronics
description: Arbitrary waveform generators and signal sources used in quantum control stacks.

name: Tabor Quantum Solutions
description: Direct RF/microwave large-scale control and measurement systems (Proteus AWG/digitizers) for quantum processors.
notes: Website corrected to taborquantumsolutions.com. Arm of Tabor Electronics (Haifa); Proteus used in the Echo-5Q system. Founded year unconfirmed.

name: Tektronix
description: Oscilloscopes, AWGs and measurement instruments used in quantum control and characterization.
notes: Fortive subsidiary.

name: Thorlabs
description: Optical components, opto-mechanics, fiber optics and photonics building blocks for quantum labs.
notes: Privately held; founded by Alex Cable.

name: TOPTICA Photonics
description: Tunable diode and frequency-stabilized laser systems for ion/atom cooling, clocks and sensing.
notes: Leading laser supplier for trapped-ion/neutral-atom quantum.

name: Tropic Square
description: Open-architecture RISC-V tamper-proof secure-element chips (TROPIC01) with on-chip TRNG/crypto.
notes: Reclassified to Components: primary product is a secure-element chip (TROPIC01).

name: Tundra Systems Global
description: Silicon-photonics all-optical computing chip developer.
notes: Founded 2014, Cardiff. Current site tsgl.xyz (record's tundrasystems.eu unreachable). Tracxn lists it as deadpooled; activity unclear.

name: ULVAC Cryogenics
description: Supplies cryocoolers and cryogenic equipment used in superconducting quantum computers.
notes: Founded 1981 (1952 was parent ULVAC). ULVAC/Edwards-CTI joint venture; cryopump and cryocooler specialist.

name: Universal Quantum Devices (UQDevices)
description: Time-tagger and coincidence-counting electronics for quantum optics, QKD and photon-correlation experiments.
notes: IQC-linked, Waterloo, ON; founded 2012 by Thomas Jennewein (corrected from 2009).

name: VACOM
description: Ultra-high-vacuum components, feedthroughs and chambers for quantum experiments.

name: Vexlum
description: Manufactures high-power VECSEL semiconductor laser systems for quantum computing, sensing and space applications.
notes: Tampere University spinout; raised EUR 10M (largest Nordic photonics seed) in Feb 2026.

name: Vivid Photonics
description: Surface-emitting super-luminescent diode (SLD) photonic light-source technology; Tyndall/IPIC spinout.
notes: Primarily AR/VR display light sources; photonics enabling tech. Tyndall/UCC (Cork) spinout; some listings cite Co. Galway.

name: Wave Photonics
description: Integrated-photonics IP, components and quantum PIC packaging services for visible-light and quantum applications.
notes: University of Cambridge spinout (Toshiba Quantum Information Group).

name: YQuantum
description: Miniaturized cryogenic hardware components (BiasTs, coldstages) for next-generation quantum computers.
notes: University of Basel spinout; based at Switzerland Innovation Park Villigen.

name: Zero Point Cryogenics
description: Dilution refrigerators and cryogenic systems that cool quantum computers to millikelvin temperatures.
notes: Edmonton, AB; grew out of U. Alberta low-temperature physics research (John Davis).

name: Zhongke Fuhai / Fullcryo (中科富海)
description: Large-scale low-temperature refrigeration, helium liquefiers and dewars supplying the quantum hardware supply chain.
notes: CAS Technical Institute of Physics & Chemistry spinout (Aug 2016); state-controlled.

name: Zurich Instruments
description: Quantum control and readout systems: qubit controllers, signal generators, lock-in amplifiers.
notes: ETH Zurich spinout; acquired by Rohde & Schwarz (2021), operates as standalone unit.

name: Aegis Quantum
description: Develops and commercializes quantum cryptography/QKD systems plus design, testing and consulting services.
notes: IQC-affiliated Waterloo startup. Canonical site is aegisquantum.com (per IQC startups page). Modality 'neutral-atom' in source was unsupported and removed.

name: Aliro Quantum
description: Quantum network design, orchestration and Entanglement-as-a-Service software (AliroNet); Harvard spinout.
notes: Harvard (NarangLab) spinout; backed by Cisco and Accenture.

name: Anametric
description: Develops chip-scale quantum photonic devices for secure quantum communication and QKD; on DARPA QuANET.
notes: Partners with SMU, AFRL, ARL; on Leidos DARPA QuANET program.

name: Arq Quantum Technologies
description: Develops rare-earth-ion quantum repeater hardware for quantum networks; part of Quantum Internet Alliance.
notes: Incorporated Sep 2025, publicly launched Mar 2026; spun out of ICFO (de Riedmatten group); website not independently verified.

name: BraneCell
description: Room-temperature all-photonic, keyless quantum repeaters and QPU for distributed networks.
notes: Founded ~2015 (Littleton, MA) by Christopher Papile. Website live but stale (copyright 2018-2023); limited recent activity signals.

name: CavilinQ
description: Builds modular cavity-enhanced photonic interconnects to network neutral-atom quantum processors.
notes: Founded 2025 by Lukin (Harvard) and Bernien (UChicago); $8.8M seed (April 2026); HQ Cambridge, MA.

name: Cisco Quantum Labs
description: Cisco develops quantum networking tech (prototype entanglement chip) and partners with IBM on a distributed quantum network by ~2030.
notes: IBM-Cisco networking collaboration announced Nov 2025; entanglement chip unveiled 2025.

name: Cohaerentia
description: Develops quantum key distribution devices; one of three Italian QKD device producers.
notes: Spin-off of Politecnico di Milano; part of QUID quantum-network consortium; supplied QKD for Italtel Quantum Secure Network.

name: Craft Prospect
description: Space systems firm developing satellite payloads (ROKS) for quantum-secured (QKD) telecommunications.
notes: Glasgow-based; leads ROKS mission and supplies QKD source for Canada's QEYSSat.

name: Delft Networks
description: Deployment-ready entanglement-based quantum networks built on solid-state diamond qubits.
notes: QuTech spinout (2024); founders incl. Ronald Hanson and Stephanie Wehner.

name: Deutsche Telekom (Quantum)
description: Deutsche Telekom researches quantum communication, QKD and quantum-safe networking for telecom infrastructure.
notes: QUTAC member.

name: Guoke Quantum Communication Network (国科量子)
description: CAS-affiliated operator building/running China's provincial QKD backbone (145 nodes, 17 provinces, satellite links).

name: HEQA Security
description: Production-grade quantum-safe appliances integrating QKD with a NIST-aligned post-quantum cryptography overlay.
notes: Formerly QuantLR; founded 2018; part of IDQ-led quantum-safe ecosystem.

name: ID Quantique
description: Pioneer of commercial QKD systems (Cerberis/Clavis), QRNG and quantum-safe networks.
notes: University of Geneva spinout; IonQ completed acquisition May 2025. Operates within IonQ networking.

name: KEEQuant
description: Integrated-photonic continuous-variable QKD systems for critical-infrastructure security.

name: KETS Quantum Security
description: Chip-scale quantum key distribution (QKD) and quantum random number generation; Bristol spinout.
notes: University of Bristol spinout; spans communication and security, primary identity QKD hardware.

name: Ki3 Photonics Technologies
description: Modular photonic hardware for scalable quantum networking and entanglement distribution over fibre.
notes: INRS spinout (Roztocki, Jestin; Morandotti lab), Montreal. Founded 2015 per multiple sources (record had 2019).

name: Kloch
description: Develops quantum-safe and QKD-based secure communication solutions.
notes: Profiled in 2025 QKD market reports alongside ID Quantique, Toshiba, etc. Limited public company detail.

name: KT Corporation
description: Telecom operator running QKD field trials and quantum-secure data links; leader in QKD international standards.
notes: Founded 1981; HQ Bundang, Seongnam. Partners with Toshiba on QKD pilots; active in Korea's quantum testbeds.

name: levelQuantum
description: Develops fiber, free-space and satellite quantum key distribution, including device-independent (DI-QKD).
notes: PoliHub/I3P incubated; NATO DIANA selectee. Woman-led; CEO Magdalena Stobinska.

name: Lightsynq Technologies
description: Diamond color-center quantum interconnect and memory startup; acquired by IonQ in 2025.
notes: Harvard/AWS CQN spinout; $18M Series A (Nov 2024); acquired by IonQ May 2025.

name: LQUOM
description: Yokohama National University spinout building quantum repeaters and entanglement sources for the quantum internet.
notes: Raised Series B; partners include Toshiba (investor) and Anritsu.

name: LuxQuanta
description: Develops continuous-variable quantum key distribution (CV-QKD) systems for ultra-secure communications.
notes: ICFO spin-off founded May 2021; closed EUR 8M Series A in Oct 2025.

name: MagiQ Technologies
description: Released first commercial QKD product (Navajo, 2003); now also optical sensing and seismic monitoring.
notes: Confirmed Somerville, MA; founded 1999.

name: memQ
description: UChicago/Argonne spinout building silicon-photonic quantum memory and network interface modules.
notes: Raised ~$10M in 2026; founded 2021.

name: Miraex
description: TFLT photonic microwave-to-optical quantum transducers/interconnects for distributed quantum computing and networking.
notes: EPFL spinout (Ecublens); acquired 100% by SEALSQ, completed June 2026.

name: Next Generation Quantum (NGQ)
description: Develops quantum optical interconnects to network multiple quantum computers; City Tech-linked patent.
notes: Founded 2019, Brooklyn (founders Raklyar/Kolmakov). PitchBook lists status 'Out of Business' though LinkedIn/ngq.io still appear; provided domain nextgenq.com unverified. Set uncertain.

name: Nokia Bell Labs (Quantum)
description: Nokia Bell Labs researches quantum networking and quantum-safe cryptography, including space-based quantum-secure optical trials.
notes: Bell Labs HQ is Murray Hill, NJ (moving to New Brunswick, NJ by 2027); parent Nokia is Espoo, Finland. Original record's Finland/Espoo was incorrect for the lab.

name: Nu Quantum
description: Quantum networking hardware and orchestration (Quantum Networking Unit) to connect QPUs; Cambridge spinout.
notes: University of Cambridge Cavendish Laboratory spinout; raised ~$60M in 2025.

name: Photarix
description: Quantum Ring single-photon LED (QR-SPLED) telecom-wavelength sources for QKD; Lancaster spinout.
notes: University of Lancaster spinout (Companies House 16544546); website verified working.

name: Pramatra Space
description: Builds entanglement-based QKD systems for satellite and terrestrial networks using photonic integrated circuits.
notes: Raised pre-seed May 2026 (Seafund). Founders Richa & Vinay Hukumchand; validated QKD chip at IIT Madras; 2027 orbital demo planned.

name: Q*Bird
description: TNO/QuTech spinout building scalable multi-user MDI-QKD hardware and software for critical infrastructure.
notes: QuTech/TNO spinout; Falqon MDI-QKD series; Benelux cross-border MDI-QKD link.

name: Qasky (问天量子 / Anhui Wentian)
description: Chinese quantum cryptography firm making QKD terminals and quantum communication core devices.
notes: Founded 2016 in Wuhu, Anhui (Cai Jiren, Guo Guangcan, Han Zhengfu) to commercialize USTC quantum-crypto research.

name: QEYnet
description: Building a global QKD network using low-cost microsatellites; CSA-funded QKD demonstration.
notes: Maple, Ontario; founded 2016. Awarded CSA funding for satellite QKD demo.

name: QRate
description: Fiber-optic quantum key distribution (QKD) systems, single-photon detectors and QRNG; Russian Quantum Center spinout.
notes: RQC spinout; site goqrate.com active with 2026 news. Clients incl. Sberbank, Gazprombank, Rosatom. QKD system reported ready for national certification in 2024.

name: QTI - Quantum Telecommunications Italy
description: First Italian quantum-telecom company; builds QKD systems, key management and quantum SDN solutions; now develops quantum repeaters.
notes: Spin-off of CNR National Institute of Optics (Florence, Arcetri); Telsy (TIM Group) is a shareholder. Founded Oct 2020.

name: qtlabs (Quantum Technology Laboratories)
description: Optical and quantum communication systems including satellite and ground-based QKD.
notes: Austrian Academy of Sciences spinout.

name: Quantized Technologies
description: UCalgary spinout building quantum-secure data exchange (QuSera) and quantum repeaters for the quantum internet.
notes: Calgary, AB; UCalgary spinout (Smith, Oblak), founded 2020 (record had blank). Primary identity is quantum communication/networking.

name: QuantLR
description: Develops low-cost, easy-to-integrate QKD hardware for secure communications.
notes: Rebranded to HEQA Security (formerly QuantLR); canonical site is now heqa-sec.com. Founded 2018, Jerusalem.

name: Quantum Bridge Technologies
description: University of Toronto spinout commercializing Distributed Symmetric Key Establishment (DSKE) for quantum-safe comms.
notes: U of Toronto spinout (Hoi-Kwong Lo, Mattia Montagna), founded 2019.

name: Quantum Generation
description: Satellite-based quantum communication and secure mesh networking venture.
notes: Site live but heavy blockchain/crypto-token marketing; independent technical validation limited. Founding year ~2018 unverified.

name: Quantum Industries
description: Entanglement-based QKD systems securing telecom and critical infrastructure against quantum threats.
notes: Founded 2023 (corrected from 2021); spun out of Austrian Academy of Sciences. Rebranding/operating as zerothird; corrected website from quantumscience.at.

name: Quantum Network Design (QND)
description: Simulation software to design, analyze and optimize quantum networks before deployment.
notes: University of Innsbruck spinout (Duer/Pirker), registered Dec 2023 in Innsbruck (corrected from Vienna). Could not confirm qnd.tech resolves; website unverified.

name: Quantum Optics Jena
description: Entanglement-based QKD systems and entangled-photon sources for quantum-secure networks.
notes: Fraunhofer IOF spinout.

name: QuantumCTek (国盾量子)
description: China's dominant QKD vendor; supplies QKD devices, key management and network gear behind the national quantum-secure backbone.
notes: USTC spinout; first quantum company on Shanghai STAR Market (2020). China Telecom became controlling shareholder in early 2025.

name: QuantumCTek-affiliated China Telecom Quantum Group (中电信量子)
description: State-owned operator delivering hybrid QKD+PQC services, quantum-encrypted lines and 5G quantum encryption.
notes: China Telecom Quantum Group established Hefei May 2023; became QuantumCTek controlling shareholder in 2025.

name: Qubitekk
description: Builds entangled-photon sources and quantum network hardware; operated EPB Chattanooga quantum network.
notes: Acquired by IonQ; deal completed January 2025 (~$54.5M).

name: quCertify
description: Provides secure quantum communication and quantum network solutions (QKD, entanglement sources, QRNG).
notes: Spin-off of Stockholm University quantum optics group (often grouped with WACQT/Chalmers); partner in Sweden's NQCIS. HQ likely Stockholm, not Gothenburg.

name: Qunnect
description: Stony Brook spinout making room-temperature quantum memories and entanglement sources; powers GothamQ NYC network.
notes: Stony Brook spinout. Demonstrated metro-scale entanglement swapping with Cisco (2026).

name: SK Telecom
description: Telecom operator deploying QKD networks, QRNG-secured 5G and quantum-secured smartphones with ID Quantique.
notes: Invested US$65M in ID Quantique (2018); QKD with IDQ; SK Broadband nationwide QKD network.

name: SpeQtral
description: Satellite-based and terrestrial quantum-key-distribution systems; delivered entanglement-based QKD payload for SpeQtre mission.
notes: CQT spinout; partners with SES.

name: Telsy
description: TIM Group cybersecurity company developing QKD and quantum-safe communication solutions.
notes: Founded 1971, Turin; part of TIM Group; QKD via subsidiary QTI.

name: ThinkQuantum
description: University of Padua spinout making terrestrial and free-space/satellite QKD platforms and QRNG devices for cybersecurity.
notes: Spin-off of University of Padua (Villoresi/Vallone); founded 2021; production in Sarcedo (Vicenza).

name: Toshiba
description: Long-running QKD program; commercializes fiber-based QKD systems and has deployed quantum-secured metro networks in London and Tokyo.
notes: QKD research began 1999 at Cambridge Research Lab; first 100km QKD in 2003. R&D in Cambridge UK and Japan.

name: VeriQloud
description: Quantum communication hardware and software (Qline QKD) for secure distributed networks.
notes: Co-founded by Elham Kashefi, Marc Kaplan, Josh Nunn.

name: Welinq
description: Neutral-atom quantum memories and interconnects (QDrive) for distributed quantum computing and networking.
notes: Sorbonne/CNRS/PSL spinout; sells commercial quantum memory.

name: Xen Quantum
description: Develops chip-scale quantum key distribution modules in silicon photonics for secure communications.
notes: Emerged from Wave Photonics; Cambridge Enterprise spinout. Backers include Parkwalk, QAI Ventures, Silicon Catalyst, Berkeley SkyDeck.

name: XT Quantech (循态量子)
description: Develops CV-QKD quantum communication systems integrated with telecom infrastructure; SJTU spinout (Zeng Guihua).
notes: SJTU-linked spinout (XunTai Quantech).

name: zerothird
description: Entanglement-based QKD and Key-as-a-Service for secure communication.
notes: Brand of Quantum Industries GmbH (Vienna); same legal entity, so this record likely duplicates the Quantum Industries record.

name: Aegiq
subsector: Full-stack photonic quantum computing & single-photon sources
description: Builds deterministic single-photon sources and a full-stack photonic quantum computing platform; U. Sheffield spinout.
notes: Sheffield, UK; also active in quantum communications/QKD; founded 2019.

name: Alice & Bob
description: Superconducting cat-qubit quantum processors targeting hardware-efficient fault tolerance.
notes: ENS/Inria-rooted; Paris and Boston; PROQCIMA-selected; NVIDIA NVentures backing.

name: Anyon Systems
description: Vertically integrated full-stack superconducting quantum computers (Qube/MonarQ), built and integrated in-house.
notes: HQ in Dorval (greater Montreal), QC; delivered Canada's first gate-based quantum computer. Canadian Quantum Champions Program awardee (CA$23M, 2025). Earlier note about a 'Singapore spinout' was incorrect.

name: AQT (Alpine Quantum Technologies)
description: Trapped-ion quantum computers in 19-inch racks at room temperature with cloud access.
notes: University of Innsbruck spinout.

name: Archer Materials
description: ASX-listed firm developing the carbon-based 12CQ room-temperature qubit chip alongside A1 Biochip products.
notes: ASX:AXE, based at Lot Fourteen, Adelaide. Quantum program from ~2018; CEO Mohammad Choucair.

name: Arque Systems
description: Silicon spin-qubit quantum computing hardware with integrated on-chip cryo-CMOS control.
notes: RWTH Aachen/Forschungszentrum Juelich (JARA) spin-off; HQ is Aachen, not Munich; canonical site arque.systems.

name: Atlantic Quantum
description: Superconducting fluxonium qubits with co-located cryogenic control electronics; MIT (EQuS) spinout.
notes: Acquired by Google Quantum AI (Oct 2025). Previously selected for DARPA QBI.

name: Atom Computing
description: Neutral-atom quantum computing; first platform to surpass 1,000 physical qubits; partners with Microsoft.
notes: Founded by Ben Bloom and Jonathan King. HQ in Berkeley with a Boulder, CO operations facility; selected for DARPA QBI.

name: AWS Center for Quantum Computing
description: AWS quantum effort: Braket cloud aggregates multiple QPUs/simulators; in-house Center develops cat-qubit hardware (Ocelot).
notes: Ocelot chip announced 2025; Braket also aggregates third-party QPUs.

name: BardeenQ Labs
description: Develops room-temperature quantum machines and spins out subsidiary quantum-tech startups ('Roivant for quantum') for Industry 4.0.
notes: Houston, TX; named after John Bardeen, led by Ayo Kolapo (Univ. of Houston). Little recent activity; room-temperature claims unverified. Site http://bardeenq.com.

name: Bleximo
description: Application-specific superconducting quantum accelerators; co-designs hardware/software for targeted problems.
notes: Founded by Alexei Marchenkov. Listed among active Bay Area firms but little recent public activity; status hard to confirm.

name: Bose Quantum / QBoson (玻色量子)
description: Builds room-temperature coherent photonic quantum computers (optical Ising machines) for optimization problems.
notes: Canonical site qboson.com (boseq.com not reachable). Raised ~$145M (CNY 1B) Series B in 2026; opened Shenzhen photonic QC factory Nov 2025.

name: Buchou Quantum (不筹量子)
description: Neutral-atom quantum computing company spun off from Fudan University (Li Xiaopeng's lab).
notes: Angel round completed late 2025. No standalone website confirmed.

name: C12 Quantum Electronics
description: Quantum processors based on electron spins in suspended carbon nanotubes.
notes: ENS Paris spinout; PROQCIMA-selected.

name: CAS Cold Atom Technology / Zhongke Kuyuan (中科酷原)
description: Builds neutral-atom quantum computers (Hanyuan series) and portable cold-atom quantum gravimeters; CAS Wuhan spinout.
notes: Canonical site cascoldatom.com (casca.cn not confirmed). Hanyuan-1 entered commercial use Oct 2025; Hanyuan-2 200-qubit dual-core system unveiled May 2026.

name: Creotech Instruments / Creotech Quantum
description: Polish maker of quantum control electronics, QKD and a domestic superconducting quantum computer for the EU.
notes: Quantum unit demerged from Creotech Instruments and IPO'd on Warsaw Stock Exchange as Creotech Quantum on 17 Apr 2026.

name: Crystal Quantum Computing
description: Trapped-ion quantum computer combining ion-crystal stability with fast Rydberg gates.
notes: Launched 2021; Telecom Paris incubated; BPI France deeptech; site is crystalqc.io (not crystalquantumcomputing.com).

name: D-Wave Quantum
description: World's first commercial quantum computer supplier; quantum annealing (Advantage) plus an emerging gate-model roadmap.
notes: NYSE: QBTS. Canadian roots, major US operations; acquiring Quantum Circuits Inc. ($550M, announced 2026).

name: Diraq
description: UNSW spinout building silicon-CMOS spin-qubit processors on standard semiconductor fabs; led by Andrew Dzurak.
notes: HQ Sydney, Australia; US labs in Palo Alto and Chicago. $38M CHIPS Act planned funding; DARPA QBI.

name: Duality Quantum Photonics
description: Integrated photonic processors and chips for quantum computing; University of Bristol-linked spinout.
notes: Canonical site is dualityqp.com (not dualityquantumphotonics.com); HQ is Bristol, not London. Co-founded by Anthony Laing/Alberto Politi.

name: e-Spin (易思旋磁)
description: Develops room-temperature 'magnetic' (spintronic) combinatorial-optimization chips; quantum-inspired approach.
notes: Quantum-inspired (not gate-based quantum). Seed round Nov 2025. No standalone website confirmed.

name: EeroQ
description: Builds quantum processors using electrons trapped on superfluid helium, a CMOS-compatible approach.
notes: HQ in Chicago. Demonstrated single-electron control above 1 K (2025, Physical Review X).

name: eleQtron
description: Trapped-ion quantum computers using microwave/RF-controlled MAGIC technology instead of lasers.
notes: University of Siegen spin-off; raised EUR 57m Series A (2026).

name: Equal1
description: CMOS silicon spin-qubit quantum chips; UnityQ system-on-chip integrates quantum and classical components.
notes: University College Dublin spinout; HQ Dublin with US/Canada/Romania/Netherlands offices. Unveiled rack-mounted RacQ system.

name: Fujitsu
description: Co-developed 256-qubit superconducting machine with RIKEN; offers Digital Annealer quantum-inspired optimizer.
notes: 256-qubit system unveiled Apr 2025 with RIKEN; targeting 1,000+ and 10,000+ qubit STAR-architecture systems.

name: Google Quantum AI
description: Google's quantum division builds superconducting processors (Sycamore, Willow) focused on error correction and supremacy.
notes: Quantum supremacy (2019) and error-correction milestone with Willow (2024). Acquired Atlantic Quantum (2025).

name: Groove Quantum
description: Germanium-based spin-qubit quantum processors compatible with semiconductor manufacturing.
notes: QuTech spinout (2024); unveiled 18-qubit spin processor in 2026.

name: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE)
description: Hewlett Packard Labs leads a quantum-HPC integration framework selected for DARPA QBI, with Qolab on superconducting HW.
notes: DARPA QBI Stage A; acts as system integrator across modalities (superconducting hardware via partner Qolab/J. Martinis).

name: Hitachi
description: R&D in silicon spin-qubit technology and a CMOS quantum-annealing chip for combinatorial optimization.
notes: Silicon spin qubit research at Hitachi Cambridge Lab; new qubit-control method extending coherence presented 2024.

name: Hon Hai Research Institute (Foxconn) Quantum Computing Center
description: Foxconn research arm running Taiwan's first trapped-ion quantum lab (opened 2023); roadmap to prototype by 2027.
notes: Trapped-ion lab opened Oct 2023; >50-ion arrays by mid-2025; partners with QunaSys on fermionic encoding.

name: Honeywell Quantum
description: Honeywell originated trapped-ion quantum hardware and remains the largest (~54%) shareholder of Quantinuum.
notes: HQS folded into Quantinuum in 2021; Quantinuum filed for IPO May 2026. Honeywell still majority owner.

name: HRL Laboratories
description: Research lab demonstrating universal control of encoded silicon (Si/SiGe) spin qubits via voltage pulses.
notes: Owned by Boeing and GM; major DARPA/IARPA spin-qubit research contractor.

name: Hyqubit (华翊量子 / Huayi Quantum)
description: Develops trapped-ion quantum processors and cloud; Tsinghua spinout from Duan Luming's group, 100+ qubit systems.
notes: Founded Jan 2022 from Tsinghua Quantum Information Center (Duan Luming). Raised hundreds of millions yuan Series A.

name: IBM Quantum
description: IBM's quantum division builds superconducting QPUs (Heron, Condor) and the Qiskit stack, targeting fault-tolerant systems.
notes: Roadmap toward fault-tolerant 'Starling' by 2029. Largest cloud-accessible superconducting fleet.

name: infinityQ Technology
description: Room-temperature quantum-analog computing hardware (infinityQube) targeting optimization problems.
notes: Founded 2020 in Montreal by Aurelie Helouis and Jean-Michel Sellier (ex-MILA).

name: Infleqtion
description: Cold-atom firm: Tiqker optical clock, Rydberg RF sensors, inertial sensing, and neutral-atom computing (Superstaq SW).
notes: NYSE: INFQ; first neutral-atom company public (SPAC, Feb 2026). Formerly ColdQuanta. Strong in quantum sensing/clocks.

name: Intel Quantum
description: Develops silicon spin-qubit chips (Tunnel Falls) and cryogenic control electronics (Horse Ridge) via its CMOS fabs.
notes: Leverages Intel's semiconductor manufacturing for scalability.

name: IonQ
description: First pure-play public quantum company; builds trapped-ion processors (Forte, Tempo) and quantum networking systems.
notes: NYSE: IONQ. Acquired Oxford Ionics (completed 2025) and chipmaker SkyWater (announced 2026).

name: IQM Quantum Computers
description: Builds full-stack superconducting quantum computers for HPC centres and research.
notes: Primary HQ is Helsinki, Finland; large Munich operations. Listed under Germany for completeness.

name: Jiuzhang Quantum (九章量子)
description: Name tied to USTC's Jiuzhang photonic boson-sampling processors; no confirmed commercial company of this name.
notes: Jiuzhang is a USTC/CAS research processor (4.0 in 2026), NOT a confirmed commercial spinout. The photonic commercial spinout from this ecosystem is TuringQ (Shanghai). No 'Jiuzhang Quantum' company or website verified.

name: Liangyi Wanxiang / Atom to Infinity (量羽万象)
description: Full-stack neutral-atom quantum computer startup spun out of Tsinghua University; first machine targeted ~2027.
notes: Correct name is Liangyi Wanxiang (量羽万象). Founded July 2024; raised >100M RMB Series A (2026). No standalone website confirmed.

name: LogicBit (逻辑比特)
description: Develops error-correctable superconducting quantum chips and QEC systems; Zhejiang University spinout.
notes: Spun off from Zhejiang University; released Mogan-1 and Tianmu-1 chips. Pre-A round Oct 2025. No standalone website confirmed.

name: MatriQ / Atomic Matrix (原子矩阵)
description: Full-stack Rydberg dynamic tweezer-array neutral-atom quantum computers; from USTC/Pan Jianwei-linked team.
notes: Seed round completed November 2025; incubated by Light Capital. No standalone website confirmed.

name: Microsoft Quantum
description: Pursues topological qubits (Majorana 1 chip, 2025) and runs Azure Quantum cloud aggregating multiple hardware backends.
notes: Majorana 1 unveiled Feb 2025. Azure Quantum partners with Quantinuum, IonQ, Atom Computing. Research roots in Station Q (2005).

name: Nanofiber Quantum Technologies (NanoQT)
description: Nanofiber cavity-QED interconnects for modular/networked neutral-atom quantum processors.
notes: Raised $14M Series A (2025). Multi-site: offices in Tokyo, Palo Alto CA, and College Park MD; often listed as a Japanese startup.

name: NEC
description: Develops superconducting quantum-annealing machines and LHZ-architecture units for fully-connected optimization.
notes: Demonstrated first solid-state qubit in 1999; LHZ unit cell with ParityQC (2022); vector-annealing and gate-based work.

name: neQxt
description: Full-stack trapped-ion quantum computing systems, hardware and software.
notes: JGU Mainz (Schmidt-Kaler group) spin-off; founded 2022, not 2023.

name: Nord Quantique
description: Fault-tolerant superconducting quantum computers using bosonic (cavity) qubits for hardware-efficient error correction.
notes: Universite de Sherbrooke spinout; ~US$1.4B valuation (Fidelity-backed, 2026). Canadian Quantum Champions Program awardee.

name: NTT (NTT Research / Coherent Ising Machine)
description: Telecom giant developing photonic Coherent Ising Machines and optical quantum computing (with OptQC/UTokyo).
notes: Signed OptQC collaboration Nov 2025 toward 1M-qubit optical computer by 2030; runs Coherent Ising Machine cloud.

name: NTT Research / NTT Quantum
description: NTT Research runs the photonic Coherent Ising Machine platform and deep quantum/optical basic research; parent NTT is Tokyo-based.
notes: NTT Research is the US arm (Sunnyvale). NTT co-developed Japan's first superconducting quantum computer with RIKEN/AIST (2023).

name: OptQC Corp.
description: Builds scalable room-temperature optical quantum computers using time-domain multiplexing (UTokyo Furusawa Lab).
notes: Founding year ambiguous (research-origin 2021; some sources cite Sept 2024 incorporation). NTT partner; targeting 10k qubits 2027, 1M by 2030.

name: Oratomic
description: Neutral-atom startup building utility-scale, fault-tolerant quantum computers; founded by Caltech/Harvard pioneers.
notes: Launched March 2026 from Caltech (Endres/Faraon) and Harvard research; team includes John Preskill.

name: ORCA Computing
description: Full-stack photonic quantum computers (PT-1/PT-2) using telecom-grade components and quantum memories; Oxford spinout.
notes: London HQ with Toronto and Austin offices; founders Ian Walmsley, Richard Murray, Josh Nunn; raised $37M Series B Nov 2025.

name: Origin Quantum (本源量子)
description: China's leading full-stack superconducting quantum computer maker; builds chips, control electronics, software and Wukong cloud machine.
notes: USTC-linked spinout in Hefei (founded 2017); operates Origin Wukong (now 4th-gen, 180 qubits) and open-sourced the Origin Pilot OS.

name: Oxford Ionics
description: Trapped-ion quantum computing with electronic qubit control on silicon chips; Oxford University spinout.
notes: Acquired by IonQ (US), deal completed Sept 2025 (~$1.075B); UK operations remain in Oxford.

name: Oxford Quantum Circuits
description: Superconducting quantum processors using its proprietary Coaxmon 3D architecture, offered as Quantum-Compute-as-a-Service; Oxford University spinout.
notes: HQ at Thames Valley Science Park, Shinfield (Reading), Berkshire.

name: Pasqal
description: Neutral-atom quantum processors and analog/digital quantum computers; going public via Bleichroeder SPAC.
notes: Co-founded with Nobel laureate Alain Aspect; PROQCIMA-selected; SPAC merger with Bleichroeder Acquisition Corp II (~$2B), expected close H2 2026.

name: Peak Quantum
description: Inherently error-protected superconducting quantum processors aimed at scalable fault tolerance.
notes: Walther-Meissner-Institute (WMI) Garching spin-off; canonical site is peakquantum.de.

name: Photonic Inc.
description: Builds networking-first silicon-spin (T-centre) quantum systems with telecom-band photonic links for distributed networks.
notes: Founded by Stephanie Simmons (SFU). ~US$2B valuation after $200M round (Canadian quantum unicorn).

name: Planckian
description: Develops superconducting quantum processors with shared-control architecture, plus quantum-battery energy tech.
notes: Canonical site is planckian.co (record had planckian.tech); spin-off of U. Pisa and Scuola Normale Superiore; ~EUR 2.7M pre-seed.

name: planqc
description: Neutral-atom quantum computers using atoms in optical lattices; MPQ spinout targeting large-scale systems.
notes: Max Planck Institute of Quantum Optics spinout; Munich Quantum Valley.

name: PsiQuantum
description: Building a fault-tolerant photonic quantum computer using silicon photonics (Omega) made at GlobalFoundries.
notes: Deployment sites in Brisbane and Chicago. Targeting a million-qubit machine.

name: Q-Factor
description: Neutral-atom quantum computing startup aiming to scale beyond one million qubits via a new system architecture.
notes: Founded by Weizmann/Technion physicists; $24M seed (NFX, TPY, Intel Capital) out of stealth Apr 2026.

name: Qarakal Quantum
description: Full-stack superconducting quantum company building QPUs and a software stack for novel gates and circuits.
notes: Website corrected to qarakal.ai. Founded ~2023 by Dr. Nissan Maskil (CEO) and Prof. Nadav Katz (CTO) with IAI/ELTA, Yissum, Omnisys.

name: QC82
description: Integrated photonic chips for room-temperature fault-tolerant quantum computing; continuous-variable approach.
notes: University of Virginia spinout (canonical site is qc82.tech, not .com); on-chip photon-number-resolving detectors.

name: QDaria
description: Early-stage startup pursuing topological (Fibonacci-anyon) quantum computing plus quantum AI software.
notes: Founded early 2025, Oslo. Currently runs algorithms on cloud hardware (e.g. IBM); own hardware is at research stage.

name: Qilimanjaro Quantum Tech
description: Builds analog and superconducting full-stack quantum computers; contributing to Spain's quantum computer at BSC.
notes: Founded 2019 (record had 2018) as spin-off of UB, BSC and IFAE; co-founder Jose Ignacio Latorre.

name: QpiAI
description: Full-stack quantum + AI firm; built India's first 25-qubit superconducting computer (QpiAI-Indus) under National Quantum Mission.
notes: Lead vehicle under India's National Quantum Mission; most-funded Indian quantum firm. HQ Bengaluru with offices in California and Finland.

name: QuamCore
description: Designs a superconducting architecture targeting one million qubits in a single cryostat via in-cryostat control logic.
notes: Founded 2022, Herzliya; $26M Series A (Aug 2025), ~$35M total incl. Israel Innovation Authority grant.

name: Quandela Quantum (Canada)
description: Photonic quantum computers from single-photon sources; Belenos (12-qubit) and Canopus (24-qubit) systems.
notes: Parent Quandela is French (founded 2017). Canadian subsidiary is 'Quandela Canada' (Montreal), launched 2024.

name: Quanfluence
description: Photonics-based optical Ising machines and quantum-inspired hardware for high-speed optimization problems.
notes: Co-founded by Anil Prabhakar; raised ~$2M seed led by pi Ventures. Mostly listed as Bengaluru, one recent source cited Pune presence.

name: QUANTier
description: Builds room-temperature neutral-atom QPUs using laser-controlled atoms, delivered via cloud.
notes: HKUST spinout (2022); backed by ParticleX and HKUST Entrepreneurship Fund. Canonical website quantier.tech could not be confirmed reachable; company is real and active.

name: Quantinuum
description: Full-stack trapped-ion company (TKET compiler) formed by merging Honeywell Quantum Solutions and Cambridge Quantum.
notes: Nasdaq IPO June 2026 (~$17.6B valuation); Honeywell majority owner. Industry-leading gate fidelities. European HQ in Cambridge, UK.

name: Quantum Art
description: Builds parallelized, reconfigurable multi-core trapped-ion quantum computers with multi-qubit gates and all-to-all connectivity.
notes: Weizmann spin-off (2022), lab in Ness Ziona; Series A extended to $140M (total ~$124M+). Demonstrated 200-ion chain.

name: Quantum Brilliance
description: ANU spinout building room-temperature diamond NV-center quantum accelerators.
notes: Australian-German; dual HQ Canberra (AU) and Stuttgart (DE European HQ). Commercial diamond foundry opened in Melbourne.

name: Quantum Circuits Inc.
description: Dual-rail cavity superconducting qubits with hardware-level error detection; Yale (Schoelkopf/Devoret) spinout.
notes: Being acquired by D-Wave ($550M, announced 2026); New Haven R&D center to continue.

name: Quantum Computing Inc. (QCi)
description: Photonic 'Entropy Quantum Computing' optimization machines (Dirac series) and thin-film lithium niobate photonic chips.
notes: NASDAQ: QUBT. Also pursues photonic sensing, LiDAR, and QRNG.

name: Quantum Fabrix
description: University of Oxford spinout building modular trapped-ion quantum hardware and atomic sources.
notes: Trades as QFX; canonical site qfx.io (record had quantumfabrix.com). At Begbroke Science Park; seed round Oct 2025.

name: Quantum Motion
description: Silicon spin-qubit quantum processors built with standard 300mm CMOS processes; UCL/Oxford spinout.
notes: London HQ; founders John Morton (UCL) & Simon Benjamin (Oxford); canonical site quantummotion.com (corrected from .tech); $160M Series C May 2026.

name: Quantum Source
description: Photonic fault-tolerant quantum computers using single atoms on photonic chips for deterministic atom-photon gates (ORIGIN engine).
notes: Website corrected to qs-labs.com. Founded 2021 (Weizmann-linked); ~$77M incl. $50M Series A; lab at Ness Ziona Science Park.

name: Quantum Transistors
description: Develops single-chip solid-state quantum processors (now diamond-based) with native photonic interconnects for data centers.
notes: Website added; HQ corrected to Herzliya; founded 2022. Received EUR 17.5M from the European Innovation Council; runs own diamond fab.

name: QuantumSphere Technologies
description: Early-stage Kenyan startup reportedly pursuing quantum hardware and software solutions.
notes: Existence unverified: only appears in a single Silicon Africa listicle, which lists a mismatched website (algeriaqcc.com). The quantumspheretechnologies.net domain redirects to a Texas IT-staffing firm (qspheretech.com), unrelated to quantum. No independent corroboration found; likely fabricated or non-operational.

name: QuantWare
description: Foundry services for superconducting quantum processors and VIO 3D vertical-interconnect chip technology.
notes: QuTech spinout (founders Matt Rijlaarsdam, Alessandro Bruno). Raised ~$178M Series B (2026).

name: Qubitcore
description: OIST spin-out building Japan's first domestically developed distributed trapped-ion quantum computer.
notes: Founded July 2024; raised JPY 1.53B seed (Apr 2026, SBI-led); OIST IP (Takahashi unit); cloud prototype targeted 2026.

name: Qudoor / Qike Quantum (国开科量子)
description: Chinese trapped-ion quantum computing firm building modular ion-trap machines plus quantum-comms devices, software and cloud.
notes: Registered as Guokaike (Qike) Quantum, Beijing; founded Jan 2019. Released Tiansuan modular ion-trap engine and AbaQ prototype. Also operates in Hefei. Canonical site qudoor.com.

name: QUDORA Technologies
description: Full-stack trapped-ion quantum computers and standalone quantum processors.
notes: Offices in Braunschweig and Hannover; building 50-qubit DLR system.

name: QuEra Computing
description: Neutral-atom quantum computers (Aquila on Braket); Harvard/MIT spinout, leader in logical-qubit error correction.
notes: Spun out of Lukin/Vuletic groups. Record logical-qubit demos; backed by Google and SoftBank.

name: QuiX Quantum
description: Room-temperature photonic quantum processors; building a universal photonic quantum computer.
notes: University of Twente spinout; uses TriPleX silicon-nitride waveguides.

name: Quobly
description: Silicon spin-qubit quantum processors using FD-SOI semiconductor manufacturing with STMicroelectronics.
notes: Formerly Siquance; CEA/CNRS-rooted (Grenoble); PROQCIMA-selected; raised EUR 115M Series A.

name: Rigetti Computing
description: Public full-stack company building superconducting tunable-transmon QPUs in its own Fab-1 (Ankaa, modular chips).
notes: NASDAQ: RGTI. Operates an in-house superconducting QPU foundry (Fab-1).

name: Rotonium
description: Builds miniaturized room-temperature photonic quantum computers using single-photon qudit (OAM) tech for edge and space.
notes: Founded 2022 in Friuli Venezia Giulia; raised ~EUR 1M seed (2024); incubated at Le Village by CA Triveneto in Padua.

name: SaxonQ
description: Mobile, room-temperature diamond NV-center quantum computers for on-site deployment.
notes: University of Leipzig spin-off.

name: SDT Inc.
description: Builds superconducting quantum computers (Kreo) and quantum-AI hybrid data centers; also photonic hardware.
notes: Opened Korea's first commercial quantum-AI data center (Gangnam, Seoul) integrated with NVIDIA via NVQLink; raised $14.1M pre-IPO.

name: SEEQC
description: Digital full-stack quantum computers using Single Flux Quantum (SFQ) control chips; operates a superconductor foundry.
notes: Spun out of Hypres in 2019. Multilayer superconductive chip foundry in Elmsford, NY.

name: SemiQon
description: Develops scalable silicon (CMOS) quantum processors and cryo-CMOS chips aimed at the million-qubit era.
notes: VTT spin-off (2023), based at Micronova, Espoo. Founders: Majumdar, Lehtinen, Prunnila, Kainlauri.

name: Silicofeller
description: Builds indigenous superconducting quantum processors; developed Kohinoor, an early Indian 4-qubit superconducting chip.
notes: Founded by Manan Narang. HQ in South Delhi (some listings show Noida). Homepage intermittently returned errors during checks but silicofeller.com is the canonical domain.

name: Silicon Quantum Computing (SQC)
description: UNSW spinout building atomically precise phosphorus-in-silicon quantum processors, led by Michelle Simmons.
notes: Backed by Australian/NSW governments, NRFC, Telstra, CBA, UNSW. 100+ staff in Sydney.

name: SmartLight Quantum (香港智光量子)
description: Develops core hardware, algorithms and system integration for optical (photonic) quantum computers.
notes: Founded July 2024; based at Hong Kong Science Park, Shatin. No standalone domain confirmed; HKSTP directory used as reference.

name: SpinQ Technology (量旋科技)
description: Builds compact NMR education quantum computers plus industrial-grade superconducting systems up to ~20 qubits, plus a chip foundry.
notes: Founded 2018 (Tsinghua-linked founders); HQ Shenzhen, lab ties to SUSTech. Raised ~CNY 600M Series C+ round (closed ~early 2026).

name: Sygaldry Technologies
description: Building quantum-accelerated AI servers combining multiple qubit modalities for fault-tolerant compute.
notes: Founded 2024 by Chad Rigetti (ex-Rigetti), Idalia Friedson, Michael Keiser; YC Spring 2025; raised $139M.

name: Taiyi Quantum (太一量子)
description: Builds ytterbium neutral-atom quantum computers; founded by former Microsoft chief quantum architect Liu Hongbin.
notes: Founded Jan 2026; ~300M yuan angel round, valuation ~CNY 1.5B. No standalone website confirmed.

name: Tokyo Quantum Computing
description: Reportedly develops integrated superconducting quantum hardware and software.
notes: Only appears in aggregator directory listings; no canonical website, funding, or primary sources confirmed. Founded year unverified.

name: TreQ
description: Designs, builds and operates bespoke open-architecture, multi-vendor quantum computing systems.
notes: QED-C member; Innovate UK Open-Architecture Quantum testbed lead (with Rigetti, Oxford Ionics, Q-CTRL).

name: TuringQ (图灵量子)
description: Develops lithium-niobate/silicon photonic quantum chips, photonic processors and supporting software; SJTU spinout.
notes: Founded Feb 2021 by Jin Xianmin (SJTU). Raised >1B yuan; among most-funded Chinese quantum computing firms; valuation near CNY 7B.

name: Universal Quantum
description: Modular microwave-driven trapped-ion quantum computers with electric-field interconnects; U. Sussex spinout.
notes: Brighton/Haywards Heath (Sussex); founded by Winfried Hensinger; holds large DLR contract in Germany.

name: Xanadu Quantum Technologies
description: Photonic quantum computers using squeezed light; maker of PennyLane open-source QML framework.
notes: Went public March 2026 via SPAC (Crane Harbor) on Nasdaq/TSX as XNDU; first pure-play photonic quantum company to list. A Canadian quantum unicorn.

name: XeedQ
description: Compact room-temperature diamond NV-center quantum computers for on-premise use.
notes: Backed by DLR Quantum Computing Initiative; offices in Leipzig and Ulm.

name: Yaqumo
description: Spinoff from Kyoto University / IMS cold-atom groups developing neutral-atom quantum computers.
notes: Quantonation's first Japanese investment (Apr 2026, $4.4M seed extension). Some sources describe it as Tokyo-based; ties to Kyoto/IMS.

name: ZuriQ
description: Scalable 2D trapped-ion (Penning micro-trap) quantum processors for improved qubit connectivity.
notes: ETH Zurich spinout; raised $4.2M seed (2025). Site is zuriq.com, not .ch.

name: 01 Communique (IronCAP)
description: IronCAP code-based post-quantum cryptography for email/digital-asset encryption and signing.
notes: TSXV: ONE; renamed 01 Quantum Inc. in Sept 2025.

name: 2pi Software
description: Software firm delivering ANU-sourced quantum random numbers as a cloud QRNG API on the AWS Marketplace.
notes: Built the AWS-hosted ANU Quantum Numbers (AQN) QRNG service in 2022. 2pi is a software consultancy; QRNG-as-a-service is the relevant offering.

name: Aires Applied Quantum Technology
description: Singapore firm advancing post-quantum cryptography; filed Southeast Asia's first international PQC/quantum patents.
notes: Company well-documented (Jan 2026 patent news) but listed website airesaqt.com does not resolve (NXDOMAIN).

name: American Binary
description: Post-quantum enterprise VPN and verified network protocol meeting NSA CNSA 2.0.
notes: HQ Kirkland, WA (was listed as Seattle); corporate entity Ambit Inc.

name: Arishti CyberTech
description: Develops quantum-safe enterprise messaging platform (MessageMe); IIT Bombay backed.
notes: Website corrected to arishti.in (not arishti.com); founder Kanak Kawadiwale.

name: Arqit Quantum
description: Symmetric key agreement (SKA-Platform) and quantum-safe encryption for telecom, government and defence.
notes: NASDAQ-listed (ARQQ) via SPAC in 2021.

name: Axion Technologies
description: Develops high-speed quantum random number generators using a patented light-scrambling technique for encryption.
notes: Founded by physicist Carol Scarlett; Luminate, Duality and Argonne CRI accelerator alum.

name: BTQ Technologies
description: Post-quantum cryptography and quantum-safe networking; designs PQC hardware accelerators and quantum-safe blockchain.
notes: Vancouver, BC; listed on Nasdaq and Cboe Canada under 'BTQ'.

name: Cavero Quantum
description: Quantum-safe authentication and key-exchange (Symmetrikey) for secure communications; Leeds spinout.
notes: University of Leeds spinout (Ben Varcoe); raised ~2.2M GBP from Foresight.

name: Chelpis Quantum Corp
description: Post-quantum cryptography, quantum-safe migration and quantum-secure chip/security solutions.
notes: Runs Asia's first Quantum Safe Migration Center (QSMC, est. 2023); achieved NIST CAVP validation for FIPS 203/204/205 in 2026.

name: ComScire
description: PureQuantum CMOS-based quantum random number generators for cryptography, lotteries and gaming.
notes: Operated by The Quantum World Corporation; Florida-based, among earliest commercial QRNG vendors (since 1994). Exact city (Gainesville) not independently confirmed.

name: Crypta Labs
description: Photonic QRNG entropy hardware for OEMs and data-centre appliances using quantum properties of light.
notes: Founded by Jose Coello; developed space-compliant QRNG for UK Space Agency.

name: Crypto Quantique
description: Quantum-driven IoT device security (QDID hardware root-of-trust, QuarkLink) using on-chip tunneling.
notes: cryptoquantique.com works; cryptoquantique.io is an alternate domain.

name: Crypto4A Technologies
description: Quantum-safe, crypto-agile hardware security modules (QxHSM) and PQC migration solutions.
notes: Founded 2017 by Bruno Couillard (ex-Chrysalis-ITS/Luna HSM); Ottawa, ON.

name: CryptoNext Security
description: Post-quantum cryptography software and toolkits to help organizations migrate to quantum-resistant security.
notes: Sorbonne/Inria spinout (Faugere, Perret).

name: Cryptosasun
description: Reported provider of AI- and quantum-enabled cybersecurity and post-quantum cryptography for finance/insurance.
notes: Listed in startup databases (Madrid, 2019, domain criptosasun.com) but live homepage and activity could not be independently confirmed.

name: Cryptosense
description: Crypto-discovery and analysis software for detecting and remediating cryptographic vulnerabilities (PQC readiness).
notes: Acquired by SandboxAQ in 2022; now part of SandboxAQ.

name: DigiCert
description: Certificate authority offering PQC-ready certificates and crypto-agile PKI for quantum readiness.

name: EigenQ
description: Post-quantum security platform spanning PQC, QRNG, quantum eSIM, quantum internet and quantum-safe networking.
notes: June 2026 definitive SPAC merger agreement with Silicon Valley Acquisition Corp (NASDAQ: SVAQ), ~$3B EV; planned Nasdaq ticker EIGQ, expected to close Q4 2026 (not yet public).

name: Elmos Semiconductor
description: Developed world's smallest QRNG chip (2026 German Innovation Award) for embedded security.
notes: Primarily automotive semiconductors (Frankfurt: ELG); entry is for its QRNG chip.

name: Encryption Consulting
description: Cryptography bill-of-materials discovery and PQC migration tooling and advisory.
notes: HQ Prosper, TX (Dallas-Fort Worth metro).

name: enQase
description: Quantum-safe security platform combining post-quantum cryptography with QRNG, QKD and digital QKD.
notes: QED-C member; spin-off of QNu Labs. Launched Feb 2025.

name: Entrust
description: Identity, PKI and HSM provider delivering quantum-safe / PQC-ready cryptographic infrastructure.
notes: Entrust Inc. founded 1994; rebranded from Entrust Datacard in 2020.

name: evolutionQ
description: Quantum-safe risk advisory and Basejump key-infrastructure suite (BasejumpSKI/QDN); co-founded by Michele Mosca.
notes: Founded 2015 by Michele Mosca and Norbert Lutkenhaus; Waterloo, ON.

name: ExeQuantum
description: PQC-as-a-Service with APIs, cloud/on-prem delivery and TLS wrapping for quantum-safe migration.
notes: HQ at Docklands, Melbourne (was listed as Sydney).

name: EYL (Essential Tech)
description: Makes ultra-small radioisotope-based quantum random number generator (QRNG) chips and IoT quantum security.
notes: ~3x3mm radioisotope-decay QRNG chip; designated under Korea's national strategic technology framework.

name: Flipscloud
description: Quantum-level encryption for cloud, IoT and big-data security.
notes: Corrected: founded 2013 in Taipei, Taiwan by Allan Ko (not Bengaluru/2020). Tracxn lists it as inactive and homepage refused connection; relevance/activity uncertain.

name: Fortanix
description: Confidential computing and data security manager with PQC integration and crypto-agility.

name: Fortytwo Labs
description: Pi-Control digital trust platform delivering quantum-safe cryptographic identity, hardware authenticators and MFA.
notes: First Indian org to earn DSCI C-SAFE recognition for an indigenous quantum-safe algorithm.

name: fragmentiX Storage Solutions
description: Quantum-safe storage combining secret sharing, post-quantum crypto and QKD.
notes: Founded 2018; developed with AIT.

name: ICTK
description: Physically Unclonable Function (PUF) chip and Q-PUF quantum security provider for IoT and smart devices.
notes: Partners with ID Quantique combining Q-PUF with QRNG.

name: InfiniQuant
description: Quantum-safe communication (CV-QKD) and high-speed QRNG technology.
notes: Hosted within Max Planck Institute for the Science of Light, Erlangen; founded 2018 confirmed.

name: InfoSec Global
description: Cryptographic discovery/agility platform (AgileSec) for crypto inventory and PQC migration.
notes: Toronto, ON; founded 2013 (record said 2016). Acquired by Keyfactor in May 2025; now operates as a Keyfactor company.

name: ISARA Corporation
description: Builds PQC solutions (ISARA Radiate, ML-KEM/ML-DSA) for crypto-agility; longtime PQC standardization contributor.
notes: Founded 2015, Waterloo, Ontario; offices in California and UK.

name: Keyfactor
description: PKI and crypto-agility platform to discover, manage and rotate cryptographic assets for PQC transition.
notes: HQ in Independence, Ohio (Cleveland metro).

name: Keypair
description: PQC and secure-element firm delivering hardware-rooted quantum-safe security for critical infrastructure.
notes: Founded 2011 (not 2020). Supplied secure-element chips for KEPCO AMI; BTQ Technologies strategic investment (2025).

name: Optalysys
description: Silicon photonic processors accelerating fully homomorphic encryption and secure-computation workloads.
notes: Optical computing; primary commercial focus on secure computation (FHE).

name: Patero
description: CryptoQoR post-quantum cryptography platform for protecting data in transit during PQC migration.
notes: HQ College Park, MD (was listed as Indianapolis); at UMD Quantum Startup Foundry.

name: Post-Quantum (Post-Quantum.com)
description: Post-quantum cryptography, secure messaging and quantum-safe identity/VPN; protects NATO comms.
notes: Founded 2009 by Andersen Cheng; code-based NIST PQC finalist.

name: pQCee
description: Post-quantum cybersecurity products including SafeQuard document signing, key management and Web3 protection.
notes: CEO Tan Teik Guan.

name: PQSecure Technologies
description: Side-channel-protected post-quantum and classical cryptographic hardware/software IP cores.
notes: Founded 2017 (was listed as 2019). Boca Raton, Florida; D.C.-area branch.

name: PQShield
description: Post-quantum cryptography hardware IP and software embedded in chips, firmware and applications.
notes: University of Oxford spinout; algorithms standardized by NIST (FIPS 203/204).

name: PQStation
description: Crypto-resiliency tools (QVision) for cryptographic discovery and migration to post-quantum standards.
notes: NTU spinout; co-founder Prasanna Ravi; published quantum-safe migration whitepaper with Mastercard.

name: PsiQre
description: Enterprise software for post-quantum cryptographic risk management and migration to quantum-safe standards.
notes: Listed as a Quantum Denmark company/partner.

name: Qabacus
description: Reportedly offers quantum encryption/security software (quantum-secure blockchain for finance).
notes: Low confidence: qabacus.com now redirects to a domain-for-sale page (HugeDomains); company activity unverified. Recategorized to Security (encryption focus).

name: Qaisec
description: Sofia startup offering quantum-resistant encryption and AI for banking and telecom, including a quantum-encrypted blockchain.
notes: CEO Boris Grozdanoff. Only Bulgarian firm on TQI's Top 25 quantum cryptography list.

name: QANplatform
description: Quantum-resistant hybrid Layer-1 blockchain implementing NIST PQC (Dilithium) signatures; EVM-compatible.
notes: Registered in Tallinn, Estonia (was listed as Budapest); Hungarian founders. Founded 2019.

name: QApp
description: Post-quantum cryptography software (PQLR SDK, Qtunnel VPN) and quantum-safe security; Russian Quantum Center spinout.
notes: CEO Anton Guglya; spun out of Alexey Fedorov's group at RQC. Based in Skolkovo, Moscow. English site en.qapp.tech active. Founding year ~2019 (not strongly confirmed).

name: QClairvoyance Quantum Labs
description: Develops a QRNG, a post-quantum VPN (QAegis QVPN) and PQC API security products on post-Moore architectures.
notes: Telangana-registered deep-tech startup (incorporated May 2024). Directors Rupali Sharma and Poosurla Sai Shankar.

name: QNu Labs
description: Indigenous quantum-safe security: QKD (Armos), QRNG (Tropos) and PQC for enterprise and government.
notes: Incubated at IIT Madras; backed by National Quantum Mission; delivered QKD systems to the Indian Navy.

name: Qrypt
description: Generates encryption keys locally from quantum entropy, eliminating key transmission; entropy-as-a-service.
notes: Sources quantum entropy from Oak Ridge and Los Alamos national labs.

name: QryptoCyber
description: PQC audit tooling that catalogs quantum-vulnerable cryptography and automates CBOM generation.
notes: Washington DC / Richmond VA area.

name: Qtonic Quantum
description: Vendor-neutral quantum cybersecurity firm offering cryptographic risk intelligence (QScout), quantum pen-testing and PQC migration.
notes: HQ Miami with R&D in Be'er Sheva, Israel. Launched independent PQC scoring platform (Qtonic Quantum Lab) at RSAC 2026.

name: QuantaX
description: Reported Nigerian startup focused on quantum encryption and cybersecurity.
notes: Existence unverified: appears only in a single Silicon Africa listicle with no city, founding year, or independent corroboration. Website quantumnigeria.com unconfirmed as this entity. Likely fabricated or non-operational.

name: QuantiCor Security
description: Post-quantum cryptography solutions for IoT, enterprise and IT-infrastructure security.
notes: TU Darmstadt spin-off.

name: Quantix Edge Security
description: Spain post-quantum and cybersecurity semiconductor design and personalization center.
notes: Joint venture (SEALSQ, WISeKey, OdinS, TProtege, Spanish SETT) created 2024; canonical site quantixedges.com (record had quantixedge.com, a different firm).

name: Quantropi
description: QiSpace platform with PQC (MASQ), symmetric encryption (QEEP) and QRNG-based entropy (SEQUR).
notes: Founded 2018 by James Nguyen and Randy Kuang; Ottawa, ON.

name: Quantum Base
description: Atomic-level Q-ID anti-counterfeiting and authentication tags verifiable by smartphone; Lancaster spinout.
notes: University of Lancaster spinout; floated on London Stock Exchange AIM in April 2025.

name: Quantum Blockchains
description: Builds quantum-safe cryptography for blockchains: patented pQKD appliance, QRNG and quantum entropy APIs.
notes: Began operations Oct 2021; demonstrated emulated QKD link between Lodz and Warsaw.

name: Quantum Dice
description: University of Oxford spinout building self-certifying high-speed photonic QRNG devices (DISC protocol).

description: Develops quantum algorithms for computational fluid dynamics, aerospace, climate and finance.
notes: Website corrected to thefirstquantum.com (firstquantum.co.kr dead). Co-founded by Prof. Doyeol Ahn; first Korean startup in IBM Quantum Network.

name: FirstQFM
description: Develops patent-pending AI foundation models for qubit calibration and error mitigation across the quantum stack.
notes: Raised EUR 1.2M pre-seed in Oct 2025 led by BSV Ventures (with Almi Invest, Luminar Ventures).

name: Haiqu
description: Quantum middleware (Rivet toolkit) improving transpilation and noise resilience to run deeper circuits on NISQ hardware.
notes: QED-C member; US-Ukrainian team. Founded by Givhan and Maksymenko.

name: Horizon Quantum Computing
description: Quantum software and automated compilers (Triple Alpha); also operates its own quantum hardware testbed.
notes: Founded by Joe Fitzsimons (2018). Went public on Nasdaq in March 2026 via SPAC merger with dMY Squared (~$503M).

name: HQS Quantum Simulations
description: Quantum simulation software for molecular and material properties in chemistry and materials.
notes: KIT spinout.

name: Huawei Cloud Quantum (华为云量子)
description: Runs the HiQ quantum simulator and quantum-algorithm research integrated with Huawei Cloud services.
notes: HiQ platform launched 2018; remains a Huawei Cloud software/simulation effort.

name: Iceberg Quantum
description: Quantum error-correction company building fault-tolerant architectures (Pinnacle) using qLDPC codes.
notes: USYD spinout (Bartlett group). $6M seed (LocalGlobe). Partners PsiQuantum, Diraq, Oxford Ionics, IonQ.

name: Inspiration-Q
description: SaaS for quantum-inspired and quantum algorithms targeting optimization, ML and finance.
notes: CSIC spin-off backed by Bullnet Capital; founding year not clearly documented.

name: Jij Inc.
description: Develops Ising-model and quantum-annealing optimization software (OpenJij, JijZept) for industry.
notes: Tokyo-based; hybrid annealing middleware and cloud optimization platform.

name: JoS Quantum
description: Quantum and quantum-inspired algorithms for finance, insurance and energy markets.

name: Kipu Quantum
description: Hardware-efficient, application-specific quantum algorithms for optimization and chemistry.
notes: Offices in Karlsruhe and Berlin; acquired the PlanQK platform from Anaqor AG (2024).

name: Kothar Computing
description: FORGE browser-based platform solving quantum many-body problems via a proprietary Quantum Symbolic Algebraic Framework.
notes: Syniad Innovations venture; launched FORGE Aug 2025; partnered with NanoAcademic on a quantum EDA suite (Oct 2025).

name: MagiQware
description: Magic-state distillation and AI-assisted optimization software for fault-tolerant quantum computing.
notes: TU Delft spin-off (2023); backed by TechScout Venture Fund.

name: Memcomputing
description: Quantum-inspired memcomputing (MEMCPU) solvers for large-scale optimization and ML.
notes: Founded 2016 at UC San Diego by Di Ventra, Traversa and Beane.

name: NanoAcademic Technologies
description: Atomistic and quantum-transport simulation software (QTCAD) for designing semiconductor qubit devices.
notes: Montreal, QC; design/simulation tooling, no own hardware, so recategorized to Software.

name: nextnano
description: TCAD software modeling quantum effects in semiconductor nanostructures, quantum dots and qubit devices.
notes: TU Munich / Walter Schottky Institute spinout. Core products nextnano++ and nextnano.NEGF.

name: nodeQ
description: Software and orchestration for quantum networks, distributed QC and quantum-safe communications.
notes: University of York spinout founded by Stefano Pirandola. Also offers PQtunnel quantum-safe product.

name: Norma Inc.
description: Quantum software (Q Platform/QPaaS) and PQC firm; building Qrion superconducting quantum computer.
notes: Canonical site is norma.co.kr (norma.kr dead). Now also commercializing Qrion hardware; partners with Rigetti, IQM. Founded 2011.

name: NVIDIA Quantum (NVAQC / CUDA-Q)
description: NVIDIA builds the CUDA-Q hybrid platform, DGX Quantum, NVQLink and the Boston NVAQC center linking GPUs to QPUs.
notes: Does not build qubits; provides GPU-accelerated simulation/QEC and quantum-classical integration. NVAQC Boston center confirmed (2025).

name: Opacity
description: Software (Quiver) for fault detection and error identification in quantum processors beyond standard benchmarking.
notes: Founded by Tim Evans (EQUS). Homepage intermittently returns Cloudflare 522 but company is active.

name: Open Quantum Design (OQD)
description: Non-profit building the world's first open-source, full-stack trapped-ion quantum computer.
notes: Non-profit founded by U. Waterloo researchers (Melko, Senko, Islam) and Greg Dick; hardware/software open-sourced. Partners include Xanadu and Unitary Foundation. Primary identity is open software/community.

name: ORIENTOM
description: Quantum software company focused on finance applications (portfolio optimization, risk, pricing).
notes: Founded 2020 by Alfred Bang (KQIA chair). Building quantum-finance middleware platform; piloted at Kookmin Bank.

name: ParityQC
description: Quantum architecture blueprints and optimization compilers (ParityOS) for problem encoding.
notes: University of Innsbruck spinout.

name: Phasecraft
description: Hardware-agnostic quantum algorithms for materials, chemistry and optimization; UCL/Bristol spinout.
notes: London HQ; also Bristol and Washington DC offices. Raised $34M Series B in 2025.

name: Piquasso
description: Open-source photonic quantum computer simulation framework (Python/C++).
notes: Developed by Budapest Quantum Computing Group / Wigner RCP and ELTE; academic open-source project rather than a commercial company.

name: Q&A Solutions
description: Reported South African quantum software startup for optimization and simulation in finance and logistics.
notes: Existence unverified: appears only in a single Silicon Africa listicle with no city, founding year, or independent source. The listicle gives a different website (quantum.solutions), and quantumsolutions.co.za is unconfirmed as this entity. Likely fabricated or non-operational.

name: Q-CTRL
description: Quantum control/infrastructure software to stabilise qubits and suppress errors; also quantum-sensing navigation.
notes: USYD spinout led by Michael Biercuk; raised ~US$113M Series B (2024). Ironstone Opal quantum navigation.

name: QBaltic
description: Develops algorithms for quantum computing, quantum and post-quantum cryptography, and AI.
notes: First Latvian QC startup; spin-off from U. Latvia, U. Tartu and Germany's QuBalt.

name: QC Design
description: Plaquette software to design, simulate and benchmark fault-tolerant quantum computing architectures.
notes: CORRECTED: HQ is Ulm, Germany (not Japan/Tokyo). Founded 2021 by Ish Dhand and Martin Plenio; customers include QuiX, C12, QUDORA.

name: QC Ware
description: Quantum/quantum-inspired algorithms and software; Forge platform and Promethium computational-chemistry product.
notes: Founded by ex-NASA Ames researchers (Matt Johnson et al.); organizes the Q2B conference series. Confirmed active 2025-2026.

name: Qedma
description: QESEM software learns each device's noise profile to suppress and mitigate errors, extending usable circuit sizes.
notes: Founded 2020 (Lindner, Sinay, Aharonov); raised $26M Series A (Jul 2025) with IBM participating; an early IBM Qiskit Function.

name: Qibo / Qrisp (Quantagonia-related)
description: Open-source high-level quantum programming framework (Eclipse Qrisp).
notes: Developed by Fraunhofer FOKUS (Berlin); now an Eclipse Foundation project. 'Quantagonia-related' label is inaccurate; not a standalone company.

name: Qinara
description: Hybrid quantum-classical software incl. Qronos, a reinforcement-learning quantum circuit optimizer.
notes: Edinburgh (Bayes Centre); early-stage. Website verified working. Founding year approximate.

name: QMatter
description: Software that compresses quantum problems to their essential core for drug discovery and materials.
notes: UCL spinout; $1.2M pre-seed Apr 2026 led by 55 North. Founders Alexis Ralli and Timothy Weaving. HQ London (Boston satellite).

name: QMill
description: Develops NISQ-era quantum optimization algorithms targeting practical quantum advantage for industry.
notes: Co-founders include Hannu Kauppinen, Mikko Mottonen and Toni Annala; uses the LUMI supercomputer.

name: QNT - Quantum Neural Technologies
description: Quantum software startup transforming high-level functional models into optimized quantum circuits; SaaS, quantum-neuron approach.
notes: Founded Nov 2021 in Athens by mathematicians A. Tsikas (CMU PhD) and T. Psarogiannakopoulos. Prior note re ex-Cambridge Quantum CTO not supported.

name: Qoherent
subsector: AI/ML for radio; quantum ML R&D
description: Machine-learning software for intelligent software-defined radios; investigates quantum ML for RF.
notes: Markham, ON. Primary focus is AI/ML for software-defined radio; quantum ML is exploratory.

name: Qomputing.AI
description: Quantum computing platform (circuit builder, 128-qubit simulator) plus education and faculty-development programs.
notes: Founded by Saurabh Sharma (IIT Bombay). Site qomputing.ai live; specific HQ city not publicly confirmed, Mumbai per prior listing.

name: Qoro Quantum
description: Middleware and orchestration software for distributed quantum and HPC integration.
notes: Canonical site is qoroquantum.net (.com returns 404). Incorporated 2 July 2024 (Companies House 15815227).

name: QPerfect
description: Tensor-network quantum emulator (MIMIQ) and logical-unit software for fault-tolerant quantum computing; now part of BTQ.
notes: Acquired by BTQ Technologies (option exercised Nov 2025, closing ~Jan 2026); Strasbourg R&D hub at CESQ. Retains distinct MIMIQ product identity.

name: Qruise
description: Machine-learning software for characterizing, calibrating and controlling quantum (and MR) devices.
notes: HQ is Saarbrücken, Germany (not London); spinout of Forschungszentrum Julich, founded late 2021.

name: QSIMPLUS
description: Develops QSIMpro, a software simulator for quantum communication systems and networks; CES 2023 Innovation Award.
notes: Website confirmed working. HQ in Seongbuk-gu, Seoul, with Daejeon research center. Also styled QSIM Plus.

name: Quanmatic
description: Quantum/quantum-inspired optimization engines for industry; deployed in ROHM chip manufacturing (2024).
notes: Founded Oct 2022 by Sumitaka Koga (CEO), Shu Tanaka (Keio), Nozomu Togawa (Waseda).

name: Quantagonia
description: Hybrid quantum-classical optimization platform (HybridSolver) for enterprise decision problems.
notes: Acquired by Strangeworks (Aug 2025); old quantagonia.com now redirects to strangeworks.com.

name: Quantastica
description: Builds software tools bridging classical and quantum computing: circuit transpilation, synthesis and conversion.
notes: Serbian origins (Subotica); incorporated/HQ'd in Finland with offices in Tallinn and Subotica. Backed by Icebreaker VC.

name: Quantistry
description: Cloud-based chemical/materials simulation platform combining quantum simulations, physics and ML.
notes: Formerly Quantum on Demand; founded 2018, not 2019.

name: QuantrolOx
description: Machine-learning software (Quantum EDGE) for automated qubit tune-up, characterisation and control.
notes: Anglo-Finnish University of Oxford spinout; HQ relocated to Espoo, Finland.

name: Quantum Benchmark
description: Error characterization, suppression and performance-validation software (True-Q).
notes: Waterloo-region/IQC spinout; acquired by Keysight Technologies in May 2021. Standalone site now redirects to Keysight.

name: Quantum Elements
description: AI-native software stack (Constellation) with a noisy-qubit simulator to accelerate building and testing quantum applications.
notes: Backed by QDNL Participations, USC Viterbi, Quantacet, Firgun Ventures. Co-founded by Izhar Medalsy, Daniel Lidar, Amir Yacoby. Also at quantumelements.ai.

name: Quantum Open Source Foundation (QOSF)
description: Non-profit supporting open-source quantum software development, education and mentorship.
notes: Globally distributed online non-profit/community; no confirmed physical HQ. Listed in Canadian Quantum Directory.

name: Quantum Rings
description: High-fidelity large-scale quantum circuit simulator and SDK running on standard classical hardware.
notes: Colorado Quantum Incubator (COQI) inaugural tenant; co-founded by Bob Wold and Venkateswaran Kasirajan. Some sources list HQ as Broomfield, CO.

name: QuantyCat
description: Cloud APIs for hybrid quantum-classical optimization across Rigetti, D-Wave, IonQ and AWS Braket.
notes: Founders Nadia Shouraboura (ex-Amazon) and Pavel Bleher.

name: QuaSi (Quantum AI for Simulation)
description: Hybrid quantum-classical models (PINNs, DeepONets) as drop-in HPC accelerators for CFD and simulation bottlenecks.
notes: Germany-based; focuses on computational fluid dynamics. Specific HQ city not confirmed.

name: QuaTI (Quantum Technology and Information)
description: Hybrid quantum-classical algorithms for extreme-weather prediction, drug development and disease diagnosis.
notes: Co-founded by Felipe Fanchini (UNESP); partners with Albert Einstein Hospital, Sao Paulo.

name: QuBench
description: Reported developer of benchmarking and performance-testing software for quantum systems.
notes: Appears only in Singapore quantum directories; website returns 404 with a self-signed certificate, so existence/activity unconfirmed.

name: Qubitor
description: Quantum computation lab/invention hub developing quantum applications, especially in machine learning and AI.
notes: Qubitor Pte Ltd, Singapore (per Quantum Computing Report). Website qubitor.com refused connection during checks; likely dormant/very small.

name: Qudyco
description: Builds QWorkbench light-matter simulation software, a quantum control box, plus education and consulting.
notes: Supported by I-HUB Quantum (IISER Pune); site shows 2024 copyright, no HQ listed.

name: Quemix
description: R&D venture developing fault-tolerant quantum algorithms (PITE) for chemistry, materials and simulation.
notes: Consolidated subsidiary of TerraSky Co., Ltd. Founded 2019; joined Quantinuum Startup Partner Program (2025).

name: Qulabs.ai
description: Quantum communication and computing software stack; demonstrated a tabletop quantum memory in India.
notes: Telangana-based; founders Vipul Mankad and Nixon Patel. Founding year cited as 2017-2018.

name: QunaSys
description: Quantum chemistry algorithms and software (Qamuy); maintains Qulacs open-source circuit simulator.
notes: Raised ~$23.7M+ (incl. $10M Series B); partners with Hon Hai (Foxconn) and pharma/chemical firms.

name: Qunova Computing
description: Quantum software for chemistry/drug discovery; HI-VQE algorithm reaching chemical accuracy on real hardware.
notes: Raised $10M Series A (2025); HI-VQE on AWS Marketplace and IBM Qiskit Functions.

name: QuSoft
description: Dutch research center for quantum software and algorithms (CWI/UvA/VU).
notes: Research center (launched 2015), not a vendor; hosted at CWI.

name: Riverlane
description: Quantum error-correction decoder stack (Deltaflow/QEC) bridging hardware and software for fault tolerance.
notes: University of Cambridge spinout, founded by Steve Brierley. Boston office; raised >$120M.

name: Semicyber
description: Small woman-owned firm developing/applying quantum algorithms and data analytics for mission-critical problems.
notes: McLean, VA; founded 2018, CEO Kayla Farrow. Holds US Air Force/AFRL STTR contracts. Small (2-10 staff); web presence limited but real.

name: Sigma-i
description: Tohoku University spinout delivering quantum-annealing optimization; major D-Wave cloud-access partner in Japan.
notes: Founded by Masayuki Ohzeki with SPARX backing; 2019 D-Wave cloud-access contract.

name: SoftwareQ (softwareQ Inc.)
description: Quantum compilers, circuit optimizers, simulators and software tools; co-founded by Michele Mosca and Vlad Gheorghiu.
notes: Waterloo-region (Kitchener), Ontario. CEO Vlad Gheorghiu; collaborating with Nu Quantum on fault-tolerant architectures.

name: Solid State AI
description: AIMS machine-learning and quantum-inspired software for advanced-manufacturing optimization.
notes: Legal entity CircuitMind Inc.; site solidstate.ai (corrected from solidstateai.io).

name: Tencent Quantum Lab (腾讯量子实验室)
description: Tencent lab researching quantum algorithms (VQE) and applying them to drug molecular dynamics and screening.
notes: Founded Jan 2018 under Shengyu Zhang; partners with AceMapAI/QureGenAI; scaled back vs peers since 2023.

name: Zapata AI (Zapata Quantum)
description: Quantum/quantum-inspired algorithm software (Orquestra) for chemistry, cryptography, pharma and ML.
notes: Ceased operations Oct 2024 after default; reemerged Sept 2025 as Zapata Quantum after bankruptcy restructuring (>$18M debt addressed). Operating again under CEO Sumit Kapur; website may redirect.
